Hedger Pressure Indicator
← Back to dashboard

Hedger Pressure Indicator

CoT hedger pressure: supply expansion & shortage stress.

As of 2026-03-10

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement (mandatory): Given the balanced hedger_deep_short_count and hedger_deep_long_count of 83 markets (1), this setup aligns with Range-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is mean reversion, as neither composite Z-score exceeds the 0.75 threshold for moderate pressure (1).
  • The signal has transitioned from a Moderate Supply Expansion regime to a "Normal" state as of the latest report (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Volatile; Threshold Proximity: Moderate; Revision Sensitivity: Unknown.

Methodology Applied:

  • Robust Z-scores above 0.75 on composite metrics indicate moderate supply or shortage pressure (1).
  • Hedger Z-score ≤ -1.0 for individual markets represents supply expansion or bearish producer pricing (1).
  • Rising shortage pressure (deep longs) implies an inflationary or expansionary economic bias (1).
  • CoT Hedger Pressure Indicator: latest observation 2026-03-10 (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the convergence of Hedger_SupplyPressure_z (0.46) and Hedger_ShortagePressure_z (0.30) toward equilibrium (1).
  • Recent stabilization follows a four-week period of supply-side expansion that peaked in late February (1).
  • Deep counts for both shorts and longs are currently identical at 83, removing the prior supply-side bias (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: Hedger Supply Pressure Z-score returning above 0.75.
  • Signal stability is classified as volatile due to the recent regime shift from "Moderate" to "Normal" within two reporting cycles (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Balanced physical market with no systemic supply or demand overhang.
  • Upside secondary: Rising physical shortages triggered by inventory depletion in key sectors.
  • Downside residual: Renewed supply expansion if producers accelerate forward selling at current prices.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Tactical (weeks); CoT positioning shifts typically guide near-term tactical setups and price discovery (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; acts as a fundamental anchor for sentiment-based signals.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the physical market supply/demand dimension of global trade (1).
  • Economic mechanism: Directional shifts imply either producer pricing power (supply) or consumer inventory stress (shortage) (1).
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Typically serves as a cross-check for speculative crowding signals like Spec vs Hedger Divergence.

Regime Context:

  • Newly re-entered "Normal" regime following a one-month deviation into supply expansion (1).
  • Direction of change: Stabilizing toward neutral fundamental conditions (1).

Model Limitations:

  • Lagging nature of weekly CoT reports and sensitivity to static z-score thresholds (1).
  • Revision window cannot be assessed from provided content.

Data & References:

  • CoT Hedger Pressure Indicator (2026-03-10) (1).
  • Influential datapoints: avg_hedger_zscore (0.0093) and hedger_deep_long_share (0.232) (1).
  • Global exchange-monitored inventory levels (e.g., LME/COMEX) would improve reliability.
